original_abstract:
The k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN) algorithm, a cornerstone of data science since 1951, remains a vital tool for data mining, recommendation engines, and the Internet of Things (IoT). Despite its longevity, traditional kNN faces significant performance bottlenecks when applied to the high-dimensional datasets characteristic of modern big data. This paper provides a comprehensive review and performance analysis of exact kNN modifications specifically designed to mitigate the "curse of dimensionality" in kNN Search and kNN Join operations.

We present an in-depth methodological assessment of 31 kNN search techniques and 12 kNN join methods, offering critical insights into their architectural strengths, inherent limitations, and practical use cases. A distinctive feature of this study is the inclusion of source code for the discussed methodologies, empowering researchers to engage in direct experimentation and comparative benchmarking. 

Bridging a recognized gap in the literature regarding exact (rather than approximate) high-dimensional kNN techniques, this survey serves as a holistic guide for researchers and practitioners. We conclude by identifying persistent challenges in the field and outlining future research trajectories, including the integration of ensemble learning and dynamic spatial partitioning to further enhance the scalability and precision of kNN in large-scale environments.
humanized_abstract:
The k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN) algorithm that was introduced in 1951 is still widely used in data science for many data mining applications such as classification, clustering, information retrieval, recommender systems, and Internet of Things (IoT) among others. Although the algorithm is very old, it is still a core component in many applications, especially when faster algorithms are not needed. One of the major drawbacks of this algorithm is that it performs very poorly and is considered to be one of the slower classification algorithms, especially in high dimensional spaces that is common with big data. This paper will give an in-depth review of all the exact kNN accelerations and the performance evaluation of these accelerated kNN algorithms for the two common kNN applications that are kNN Search and kNN Join.

This survey provides an in-depth methodological analysis of 31 kNN search algorithms and 12 kNN join methods, highlighting their structures, characteristics, and applicable scenarios. In contrast with existing literature that only focuses on algorithmic aspects, we in addition provide the corresponding source code for each methodology to enable the scientific community to implement and compare them against actual data.

Recent high-dimensional approximate kNN algorithms provide exact solutions to the kNN search problem only for low dimensions. High-dimensional approximate kNN algorithms have not been surveyed in a single article yet. This survey aims at bridging the literature gap and serving the research and practicing communities in one stop. We summarize the recent exact high-dimensional kNN algorithms, discuss the open problems in this domain and conclude with the future directions in this research area. In particular, we plan to investigate ensemble kNN and dynamic spatial partitioning techniques to enhance the performance of kNN search in the big data age.

document.input:
The k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN) algorithm, a cornerstone of data science since 1951, remains a vital tool for data mining, recommendation engines, and the Internet of Things (IoT). Despite its longevity, traditional kNN faces significant performance bottlenecks when applied to the high-dimensional datasets characteristic of modern big data. This paper provides a comprehensive review and performance analysis of exact kNN modifications specifically designed to mitigate the "curse of dimensionality" in kNN Search and kNN Join operations.

We present an in-depth methodological assessment of 31 kNN search techniques and 12 kNN join methods, offering critical insights into their architectural strengths, inherent limitations, and practical use cases. A distinctive feature of this study is the inclusion of source code for the discussed methodologies, empowering researchers to engage in direct experimentation and comparative benchmarking. 

Bridging a recognized gap in the literature regarding exact (rather than approximate) high-dimensional kNN techniques, this survey serves as a holistic guide for researchers and practitioners. We conclude by identifying persistent challenges in the field and outlining future research trajectories, including the integration of ensemble learning and dynamic spatial partitioning to further enhance the scalability and precision of kNN in large-scale environments.
